title: Sarcanthus ridleyi
taxonomy: Sarcanthus ridleyi is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. This plant is further categorized as a genus within the subfamily Cranichideae. Sarcanthus ridleyi is a member of the tribe Goodyerinae. It is native to Southeast Asia and is a type of terrestrial orchid. The classification of Sarcanthus ridleyi reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the orchid family.

intro: Sarcanthus ridleyi is a species of plant in the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the Ridely's orchid. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments. Bioactive constituents of Sarcanthus ridleyi include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal preparations, Sarcanthus ridleyi is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which are traditionally used to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
